entries.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
entries.notes: Existing ES install/config skills are the source of truth.

entries.name: Splunk Security Essentials
entries.notes: Search-tier app install and setup checklist validation.

entries.name: Splunk SOAR
entries.notes: Renders and runs SOAR On-prem unprivileged single-instance and cluster installers (Postgres + GlusterFS + Elasticsearch + HAProxy), drives SOAR Cloud onboarding checklists, installs Automation Broker on Docker/Podman, and installs the Splunk-side apps (6361, 3411). SOAR Cloud tenant provisioning and licensing remain Splunk-managed.

entries.name: Splunk App for SOAR Export
entries.notes: Optional Splunk platform to SOAR export app, handled by splunk-soar-setup without installing a SOAR server.

entries.name: Enterprise Security Native SOAR
entries.notes: ES Premier native SOAR capability. ES configuration owns pairing, response workflow readiness, and automation history review; the SOAR runtime, Cloud onboarding, Automation Broker, and Splunk-side SOAR apps remain handled by splunk-soar-setup.

entries.name: Splunk User Behavior Analytics
entries.notes: Standalone UBA is end-of-sale as of 2025-12-12 with end-of-life/end-of-support on 2027-01-31; skill handles readiness, Kafka app, and ES Premier UEBA migration guidance.

entries.name: Splunk Attack Analyzer
entries.notes: Installs app and add-on, prepares index and dashboard macro, then validates handoff state.

entries.name: Splunk Asset and Risk Intelligence
entries.notes: Installs restricted ARI app package, prepares ARI indexes, validates KV Store/roles/capabilities/readiness, and covers post-install, data source, metric, response/audit, investigation, ES integration, ES 8.5+ Exposure Analytics, ARI Add-on, ARI Echo, upgrade, and uninstall-prerequisite handoffs.

entries.name: Splunk Mission Control
entries.notes: Bundled ES 8.x component.

entries.name: AI Assistant in Enterprise Security
entries.notes: ES Cloud Security AI Assistant capability. ES configuration handles documented es_ai_settings readiness and support/account-team handoff; the generic Splunk_AI_Assistant_Cloud app route stays available for non-ES Splunk AI Assistant onboarding.

entries.name: Exposure Analytics
entries.notes: ES capability; ARI integration routes here.

entries.name: Detection Studio
entries.notes: ES detection lifecycle capability.

entries.name: Splunk Threat Intelligence Management Cloud
entries.notes: ES threat intelligence workflow.

entries.name: Splunk Cloud Connect
entries.notes: ES cloud integration readiness.

entries.name: Federated Analytics
entries.notes: Routes Amazon Security Lake / OCSF Federated Analytics setup to the federated search skill, then back to ES configuration for ASL macros, ESCU detections, and ES 8.x threat detection readiness.

entries.name: DLX
entries.notes: ES packaged support component.

entries.name: Enterprise Security Premier UEBA
entries.notes: Modern UEBA capability in Enterprise Security Premier; standalone UBA migration target.

entries.name: Splunk ES Content Update
entries.notes: First-class render-first ESCU setup and content activation review; ES mutations remain delegated to Enterprise Security configuration.

entries.name: Splunk App for PCI Compliance
entries.notes: Render-first PCI app readiness, CDE index/macro intake, CIM prerequisites, roles, reports, and installer-selection handoffs.

entries.name: InfoSec App for Splunk
entries.notes: Render-first InfoSec install readiness, source checklist, dashboard/macro/data-model checks, Cloud IDM note, and lookup handoffs.

entries.name: Splunk Common Information Model
entries.notes: Routes to the existing CIM data-model setup skill; do not create a duplicate CIM skill.

entries.name: Splunk App for Lookup File Editing
entries.notes: Render-first Lookup File Editing install readiness, CSV/KV Store inventory, SHC allowRestReplay runbook, and app health handoffs.

entries.name: Splunk AI Toolkit / MLTK
entries.notes: Not a security portfolio product, but referenced by ES detections that use ML and by selected security analytics workflows. Routes to the AI/ML skill for PSC dependency selection, AI Toolkit validation, anomaly workflows, and model inventory.

entries.name: Splunk App for Data Science and Deep Learning
entries.notes: AI Toolkit extension for external custom model runtimes, JupyterLab notebooks, Docker/Kubernetes/OpenShift/HPC/GPU handoffs, and model governance.

entries.name: Splunk App for Anomaly Detection
entries.notes: Legacy anomaly apps are audit and migration-only. New anomaly workflows should route to current Splunk AI Toolkit assistants and Cisco Deep Time Series anomaly detection readiness.

entries.name: Splunk Automation Broker
entries.notes: Docker/Podman host readiness and handoff only.

entries.name: Splunk App for Fraud Analytics
entries.notes: Render-first Fraud Analytics readiness, ES/RBA prerequisites, Lookup File Editing dependency, use-case intake, and correlation-search review.
